title: Fftssfft
content:

{{Quote|This must be the Land of the dandelion warriors. Now I've got to find a star urchin. I guess this is the place|Wicket W. Warrick|<ref name="E:TSD" />}}
'''Fftssfft''', also known by the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] as "'''dandelion warriors'''," were a [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sapient]] [[Plant/Legends|botanical]] [[Species/Legends|species]] characterized by their thicket of sharp [[Color/Legends|yellow]] quills. They grew in a [[Land of the dandelion warriors|meadow]] on the surface of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], east of the moon's [[Great Forest]]. Like all vegetal species, the fftssfft had roots that conveyed [[Water/Legends|water]] and nourishment to the rest of their bodies, but they were also able to move about. They protected their grasslands with great ferocity—when they detected movement on their territory, they used the strange quills that covered their heads by hurling them at trespassers. Their reason for killing, however, was purely territorial, and the fftssfft would usually remain immobile in the absence of intruders.

The Ewoks of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], who lived to the West of the grasslands, usually avoided crossing the territory of those they had nicknamed "dandelion warriors." However, in [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], the ffsstfft fought off what they perceived as a home invasion, when the [[Gupin/Legends|Gupin]] [[Mring-Mring]] set foot on their territory. Shortly afterwards, an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] named [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]]—who was in search of a [[star urchin quill]] that would help him cure his dying [[Parent/Legends|father]] [[Deej Warrick]]—into the dandelion warriors' high grasses and freed the Mring-Mring, which provoked an aggressive response from the plant-like beings. The Ewok and his new Gupin companion escaped unhurt from the incident.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|But dandelions can't move. They're only ''plants''.|Wicket Wystri Warrick, making erroneous assumptions about the fftssfft|<ref name="W&DW" />}}
[[File:Dandelion_warriors.png|thumb|left|250px|A group of fftssfft awaken at the sight of intruders]]
A [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the fftssfft belonged to the native [[Plant/Legends|flora]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]],<ref name="COE" /> a lush [[Moon/Legends|moon]] orbiting a [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Endor (planet)/Legends|same name]].<ref name="EAWGG">[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> Those animated plants grew in a [[Land of the dandelion warriors|meadow]]<ref name="W&DW" /> located in the leading edge of the Endor grasslands, to the East of the [[Great Forest]]. Being botanical creatures, the fftssfft drew all the sustenance they needed directly from the soil of their meadow through their roots.<ref name="COE" /> However, while most plants grew in a permanent site, some were capable of movement when they needed better soils or light,<ref name="AE">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> and the fftssfft were among them.<ref name="W&DW" />

From a distance, the fftssfft appeared to be ordinary, over one [[Meter/Legends|meter]] tall<ref name="COE" /> plants with [[Color/Legends|blue]]<ref name="E:TSD" /> or green stems sporting yellow inflorescences. In the [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] of uninformed [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], a race of diminutive beings native to Endor, they looked beautiful and hardly dangerous when observed from afar. However, when they needed to defend their territory, the fftssfft emerged from their lethargy. Their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] flashed open, their [[Limb/Legends|limb]] extended from their thick body-stems, and they started walking towards the intruders.<ref name="W&DW" /> On closer inspection, one could see that the fftssfft were roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] in structure, with a tall stem as a torso, a head that bore a tufty corolla, two shoots used as arms and two spindly roots used as feet. Their "faces" featured a pair of glowing red<ref name="E:TSD" /> or pink eyes<ref name="W&DW" /> and a beak-like appendage. When the fftssfft remained immobile in the grass, they wore their petals down like a conical hat or an [[Umbrella/Legends|umbrella]]. But as soon as they adopted an alert posture, the quills rose on their heads, forming an [[Egg/Legends|egg]]-shaped thicket of spines.<ref name="E:TSD" />

Their fighting skills, however, did not rely on their ability to walk, since they were slow movers. Once they were close enough to their victim, the fftssfft would stop and lower their heads to aim the target before firing off a bundle of their petals.<ref name="W&DW" /> Those yellow petals were in fact sharp quills that could injure or even kill those whom they hit. One out of every one hundred quills was envenomed. That "[[star urchin quill]]" would break open when it struck flesh, taking a distinctive starburst shape and releasing its venom into the victim's [[Blood/Legends|blood]].<ref name="COE" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:Thicket_of_fftssfft.png|thumb|250px|A thicket of fftssfft standing still in the meadow]]
The fftssfft were very territorial creatures, and they defended their growing land with ferocity. However, they only attacked other beings when they felt their territory was threatened,<ref name="COE" /> and they would usually stand still when nobody came around. They were sentient enough to make ropes and use them to bind their enemies.<ref name="E:TSD" /> The fftssfft also possessed [[Fftssfft language|their own]] [[Language/Legends|language]], which required no phonatory organs. It consisted of hissing sounds produced by forcing air through tiny pores in their stems.<ref name="COE" /> The [[Bear/Legends|bear]]-like Ewoks feared the fftssfft and christened them "dandelion warriors" in reference to their bellicose attitude.<ref name="E:TSD" /> Because of the dandelion warriors' fierce presence in the grasslands of Endor, few Ewoks ever crossed the area to reach the neighboring land of the [[Gupin/Legends|Gupins]].<ref name="COE" /> However, despite their redoubtable natural defenses, the fftssfft suffered from predation by other species like the brutish [[Grass Trekker/Legends|Grass Trekkers]] from the [[Desert of Salma/Legends|Desert of Salma]].<ref name="COE" />

==History==
In the year [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="BTM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref> the land of the dandelion warriors was visited by a [[Gupin/Legends|Gupin]] named [[Mring-Mring]], a gnome-like [[Changeling/Legends|changeling]] who had been [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from his people's [[Gupin stronghold|fortress]]. The angry ffsstfft caught the trespasser and tied him with a rope to a [[Tree/Legends|tree]] stump that stood in their meadow. Shortly afterwards, the dandelion warriors detected another intrusion, when the Ewok [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] set foot on their territory in search of a star urchin quill he needed to cure his ailing [[Parent/Legends|father]] [[Deej Warrick]]. While Warrick tried to untangle the captive Gupin, the ffsstfft reacted and moved into attack position, ready to hurl their sharp quills at both outsiders. However, the trespassers escaped the encounter unharmed.<ref name="E:TSD" />

==Ffsstfft in the galaxy==
{{Quote|High risk! High reward! Be a gladiator in the Big Game!|Advertisement for ther Big Game|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 19">[[Star Wars (1977) 19|''Star Wars'' (1977) 19]]</ref>}}
Although the ffsstfft were only semi-sentient,<ref name="COE" /> some had found their way offworld by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. They took part in the [[The Big Game|Big Game]], a daily [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] fight staged by Administrator [[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade]] to amuse the guests on the luxury [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]]. In that context, the ffsstfft were considered to be among the best warriors in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], on par with much bigger races such as the [[Catuman|Catumen]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] or the [[Bitthævrian/Legends|Bitthævrians]].<ref name="Blog">{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-1|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The fftssfft made their first appearance in ''[[To Save Deej]]'', the fourth episode in the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated television series. In the episode, which was written by [[Bob Carrau]], they were only referred to as "dandelion warriors."<ref name="E:TSD" /> Their native name was only revealed in ''[[Castaways of Endor]]'', an article written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and illustrated by [[Robin Pronovost]] for [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']].<ref name="COE" /> In [[2013]], they were briefly mentioned in Part 1 of ''[[Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel]]'', a series of articles written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]] for the official [[StarWars.com]] blog.<ref name="Blog" />

In real life, "dandelion" is the vernacular name of the [[Wikipedia:Taraxacum officinale|''Taraxacum officinale'']], a wild plant with yellow flowers and a characteristic "puff-ball" seedhead.<ref name="Tech Weed">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ppws.vt.edu/scott/weed_id/tarof.htm|text=Dandelion: ''Taraxacum officinale''|work=[http://www.ppws.vt.edu Virginia Tech Weed Identification Guide]|archivedate=20130512134652}}</ref>
